title: Add credentials to buildah from
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

This adds the --creds parameter to the 'buildah from' command and stubs in a few other places.  Those places that need further work are marked with '// TOM: todo'.   The plan is to fix those up in a followup PR.  This new functionality does not have any tests for it yet, I'd like to do that as a follow up too, but can be persuaded otherwise.

Please don't merge this until I take another look in the morning and give it my LGTM.  The eyeballs are a bit glossy at the moment.

comments.body: Does docker have commands like this?  Or is docker just relying on docker login?  Should we add a buildah login?

I'll let @nalind correct me if I'm wrong, but I don't believe docker has a --creds or similar param.  From what I've seen they rely on docker login.

Also FWIW, if you're pulling from a private docker registry with buildah, you can drop the --creds param if you first login with docker login.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Tests are failing.
@runcom PTAL

I'm going to have to bother @nalind on Monday for this one.  It looks like a couple of files in vendor are empty and I'm suspecting they shouldn't be.  There's also a warning in the code, but that's a chunk of code I stole from skopeo and I don't think it's the cause for the failure.  It all compiles fine in my sandbox.  Here's the error from Travis:

make[1]: Leaving directory `/home/travis/gopath/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah/docs'
can't load package: package github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otadmin: 
vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otadmin/admin.go:1:1: expected 'package', found 'EOF'

can't load package: package github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otremote: 
vendor/github.com/ostreedev/ostree-go/pkg/otremote/remoteadd.go:1:1: expected 'package', found 'EOF'
cmd/buildah/from.go:82:2:warning: ineffectual assignment to credsContext (ineffassign)
make: *** [validate] Error 1
Hmm, might be that assignment after all.  Need to dig further to see why that's a problem here but not skopeo.
LGTM.  Fingers crossed the test will pass.  I learned a nuance in golang with the error, will see if we can get our makefile to replicate/flag the issue Travis flagged.

Are we going to need the same code added to kpod pull?  If so could you open a pull request on it?
LGTM
@nalind PTAL
I thought I saw a PR for kpod pull --creds fly by, but I'm not seeing code for that.  I'll create an issue, assign it to myself and will work on that.
This needs tests.

Yep, definitely needs tests.  I've been mulling over how to create a private registry "on the fly" in a test script.  I may need to get a cert to do that and that's been the hang up, but I've not spent a ton of time on it yet.  Was hoping to get un/pwd auth working for the three buildah commands first, then get the tests going for that.

Looks like we'll need to discuss which of the other authentication related flags I should fully enable too.
@runcom PTAL

> @runcom PTAL

back from PTO, I'll take a look at this today
looks ok overall but we shouldn't leak credentials where we aren't supposed to send them
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 94f2bf0) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 265d2da) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@TomSweeneyRedHat needs a rebase.

I had to mostly gut the test for Travis to run.  I commented out most of the functionality.  At times it would complain that it could not resolve localhost, other times socket connections issues talking to dockerd, other times docker not being present. If possible I'd like to get this in as is and try to figure out what the heck the issues are in Travis/ubuntu.  These tests with the comments removed work fine on my Fedora vm.
Yes lets get this merged without a test, and manually test it for now.
@runcom @aweiteka @nalind PTAL
Can you just add a smoke test by creating a private image on the Docker hub and do a buildah from that image? LGTM otherwise

I actually started out with testing against dockerhub.com, but went to the private registry as I didn't think I could get a certificate associated with it (maybe I can, I'll relook), it didn't appear to be as flexible, and I wasn't real hip to expose my un/pwd in the test.  But perhaps I can set up a test user there for this and at least have a test in play to show that at least the creds piece works.
Only if you can do a readonly registry.   Could we setup something at crio-io or some other site that was readonly and only allowed people to pull with credentials?
IDK about the crio-io site.  Maybe?  Can we run docker on the system where the site lives?

Seems we need an external site with a readonly registry,  So that we could hard code the password. Otherwise we need to hack up something to start a registry inside of a VM/COntainer and have the test talk to it.
@nalind PTAL
As soon as we get this in should we build a new buildah or wait for @nalind big patch?
I'd like to wait for Nalin's magic #187 unless it's weeks out.
Should the changes in `util/util.go` still be there?
Got a lot of new extra whitespace at the end of lines in `from.bats`.
Make sure if we have extra config for certs, that this is handled in containers/image rather then buildah, so we don't end up with different creds for buildah, kpod, skopeo and CRI-o.
Oh shoot yes, changes to util/util.go should be backed out.  Fixing that now.  TYVM
@nalind I think I killed all the extra whitespace in from.bats.  Would love to know how you eagle eye that.
LGTM, I believe I've addressed all comments.  Please holler if not.

As an FYI, I thought I'd found a way to get a docker registry up an accessible (#247) but buildah from is not happy talking to it from within Travis when I merged it here.  That's what the pushes from today were all about.  I've put the test files back to the way they were Friday night, no other changes.
One last change in the name of `systemContextFromGlobalOptions`, then LGTM.

LGTM, merging.
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit 9ce9984 has been approved by `nalind`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=nalind 9ce9984aa9e7e2c53cc513316fdb217c70f780eb -->
:hourglass: Testing commit 9ce9984 with merge 498f0ae...

:sunny: Test successful - [status-redhatci](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/498f0ae9d700f5ee08c6e4a6c64a8ed1fdd3e666.1503428994832779520/index.html)
Approved by: nalind
Pushing 498f0ae9d700f5ee08c6e4a6c64a8ed1fdd3e666 to master...
Excellent, This only handles it in one direction, we need support for credentials in buildah push, correct?  Once we have that we can cut a new release.

Yep, just one direction with this one.  Next up is commit and push.  William Henry brought up that we might want to add creds to Inspect as well.  I'd not considered that, but think he's probably right.  I'm going to do commit/push next for sure, will look at inspect but may do a separate PR for that.
I agree lets get push/commit done, so we can cut a new version.  Need to get @nalind to remove --transport also ...
